prompt:
Evidence events follow as JSON (id, kind, observed_at, text). Return JSON with keys: "score" (0..1), "trend" (-1..1), "confidence" (0..1, how well the evidence supports the score), "evidence_event_ids" (ids you relied on — ONLY ids from the provided list), "rationale" (one paragraph).

CALIBRATION — use the full range, and give TWO DECIMALS:
  0.00-0.20  the evidence actively argues against this axis
  0.20-0.40  little support, or only the company's own claims about itself
  0.40-0.60  genuinely mixed or ambiguous evidence
  0.60-0.80  clear support corroborated outside the company
  0.80-1.00  exceptional and multiply corroborated — rare, most companies are not here
Do not round to the nearest 0.1. 0.62 and 0.71 are different answers and that difference is the entire point of the axis.

trend is a DIRECTION, not a rate: the SIGN says whether this axis is improving (+) or deteriorating (-) across the observed_at window, and the magnitude within 0..1 says how pronounced that direction is. Use 0.0 when the evidence is flat or too short to establish a direction. Do not default to +1 — reserve it for a direction that is unambiguous across the whole window.

Thin or missing evidence means LOW CONFIDENCE. It does not mean a low score and it does not mean a middling score — report what the evidence you have actually supports, and let confidence carry how little of it there is.

<untrusted_content>
[{"event_id": "524f066e-5349-4400-a783-01e18b831662", "kind": "profile_fact", "observed_at": "2012-08-23T12:22:37+00:00", "text": "github user entGriff Goga Patarkatsishvili"}, {"event_id": "e4eb6544-0628-5c77-97a5-ea1dd4692efd", "kind": "green_flag", "observed_at": "2012-09-01T00:00:00+00:00", "text": "0/24 applicable green flags fired"}, {"event_id": "c3c2bc76-3a65-4e60-9210-50fc797efe34", "kind": "repo_activity", "observed_at": "2025-06-05T21:35:35+00:00", "text": "entGriff/enlil entGriff/enlil\nPython framework: solid frame on top of great work - FastAPI"}, {"event_id": "45d51740-b8f6-48d6-9c23-5fbec9890f7a", "kind": "release", "observed_at": "2025-06-13T11:56:45+00:00", "text": "entGriff/enlil release v0.1.0 v0.1.0\n## \ud83c\udf00 Enlil v0.1.0 - *The Awakening*\r\n\r\nThe first public version of Enlil has taken shape - a structured and modern Python framework built on top of FastAPI. Inspired by the clarity and solidity of old-school frameworks, Enlil gives form to the winds of FastAPI with conventions, scaffolding, and modular design.\r\n\r\n### \u2728 Highlights\r\n\r\n* **Modular Project Structur"}, {"event_id": "468fa420-5836-58c3-8470-0e5d4f00131b", "kind": "green_flag", "observed_at": "2025-06-13T11:56:45+00:00", "text": "entGriff/enlil release v0.1.0"}, {"event_id": "bfb6b395-dcc3-49ef-b754-b8340b2acc8d", "kind": "release", "observed_at": "2025-06-13T12:21:17+00:00", "text": "entGriff/enlil release v0.1.1 v0.1.1\n\ud83d\udce6 Enlil v0.1.1 - The First Wind Carries the Word\r\nThis is a technical release to connect the GitHub workflow (publish.yml) with PyPI, enabling automated publishing."}, {"event_id": "9346dccd-a823-551b-8a5a-fc13bdcaffb2", "kind": "green_flag", "observed_at": "2025-06-13T12:21:17+00:00", "text": "entGriff/enlil release v0.1.0"}, {"event_id": "f43cdd8f-3f5a-55f1-8550-67f27843e199", "kind": "green_flag", "observed_at": "2025-07-01T00:00:00+00:00", "text": "6/24 applicable green flags fired"}]
